models.description: OpenAI's first voice model with GPT-5-class reasoning for live voice interactions, supporting parallel tool calls, adjustable reasoning effort, and 128K context window for complex conversations.

OpenAI's live translation model that translates speech from 70+ input languages into 13 output languages while keeping pace with the speaker, designed for real-time multilingual communication in customer support, education, and other interactive settings.
OpenAI's streaming speech-to-text model with a 128K context window, designed for real-time transcription of live speech with reduced hallucinations compared to prior Whisper versions.

OpenAI's most capable image generation model yet, designed for complex visual tasks with precise, usable images featuring stronger editing, better layouts, improved text rendering, and reliable instruction-following, available in the API and Codex for production workflows.

A cost-optimized, multimodal image model from OpenAI that accepts text and image inputs and produces image outputs. It is positioned as a smaller, cheaper sibling to OpenAI's full GPT-Image-1 family — designed for high-throughput production use where cost and latency are important constraints. The model supports text-to-image generation, image editing/inpainting, and workflows that incorporate reference imagery.

OpenAI's flagship video generation model creating richly detailed, dynamic video clips with synchronized audio from natural language prompts or images, featuring advanced physics simulation and multi-shot coherence .

OpenAI's flagship video+audio generation model generating short, highly realistic video clips with synchronized dialogue, sound effects, and stronger physical/world simulation than previous video models, tuned for higher visual fidelity and complex shots

OpenAI's speech-to-text model built on the GPT-4o foundation, designed for high-accuracy transcription of audio input with improved Word Error Rate compared to Whisper models, supporting multilingual transcription and optimized for real-time applications like call centers and live captioning .
A speech-to-text model that uses GPT-4o mini to transcribe audio, offering improvements to word error rate and better language recognition and accuracy compared to original Whisper models.
OpenAI's latest chat model as of May 2026, likely routing to the most advanced available version with enhanced reasoning, tool use, and multimodal capabilities .

OpenAI's flagship agent for autonomous, multi-step research workflows, leveraging advanced reasoning capabilities to plan, search, analyze, and synthesize information across diverse web sources to deliver expert-level, citation-rich reports within minutes.
OpenAI's faster, more affordable deep research model optimized for complex, multi-step research tasks, capable of searching and synthesizing information from the internet and private data sources .

A specialized model for computer-related tasks, including text, video, audio, transcription, and text-to-speech capabilities. Supports a unknown token context window. Handles Text, Video, Audio, Transcription, Text-to-Speech inputs and outputs. Supports fine-tuning for custom applications. Supports tool use for advanced automation.
Anthropic's most capable widely released model, built for the most demanding reasoning and long-horizon agentic work with adaptive thinking always on and safety classifiers for general use.

Anthropic's most advanced frontier AI model with unrestricted access to cybersecurity capabilities, available only through Project Glasswing to vetted partners. Shares identical architecture with Claude Fable 5 but lacks safety classifiers that route high-risk queries to Opus 4.8.
Anthropic's most capable generally available model for complex reasoning, long-horizon agentic coding, and high-autonomy work with adaptive thinking and dynamic workflows capabilities
Anthropic's flagship Opus model optimized for complex reasoning, agentic coding, and high-resolution vision with adaptive thinking and tool use capabilities.
Anthropic's flagship model with adaptive thinking, 1M token context window, and agentic coding capabilities
Anthropic's flagship Opus-class model for complex reasoning, coding, and agentic workflows with enhanced token efficiency and new 'effort' control.

Anthropic's flagship hybrid reasoning model released August 2025, offering enhanced real-world coding, agentic research, and extended thinking capabilities with a 200K token context window and 32K output limit.

models.model_name: Claude Opus 4 (retired, except on Google Cloud)
models.description: Anthropic's most powerful model for complex reasoning and agentic coding, leading on SWE-bench and Terminal-bench benchmarks. Supports extended thinking, tool use, and vision input.
Anthropic's mid-tier model with improved coding, computer use, long-context reasoning, and agent planning, featuring a 1M token context window and adaptive thinking.
Anthropic's mid-tier model optimized for coding, agentic workflows, and extended autonomous operation with hybrid reasoning and 200K context window.

models.model_name: Claude Sonnet 4 (retired, except on Bedrock and Google Cloud)
models.description: Claude Sonnet 4 is Anthropic's balanced model with strong coding and reasoning capabilities, improved instruction following, and extended thinking with tool use. It supports vision input and tool-based workflows, ideal for everyday coding, tool use, vision tasks, and multi-step reasoning.

Anthropic's fastest and most cost-efficient Claude 4.5 model, released October 15, 2025, designed for real-time applications, chat assistants, customer service agents, and pair programming with near-frontier reasoning and coding quality at one-third the cost of Sonnet 4.

models.model_name: Claude Haiku 3.5 (retired, except on Bedrock and Google Cloud)
models.description: Anthropic's fastest model in the Claude 3.5 family, engineered for speed and cost-efficiency while maintaining strong performance across coding, tool use, and reasoning tasks. It surpasses the previous generation's Opus model on many benchmarks at a fraction of the cost.

Claude Opus 4.7 is Anthropic's flagship model with a 1M-token context window, adaptive reasoning, and 3.3x vision resolution for enterprise-scale agents. It excels in coding, agentic workflows, and complex reasoning tasks with improved autonomy and instruction following compared to Opus 4.6.

Google's Gemini 3 Flash Preview combines Pro-level reasoning with Flash efficiency, optimized for agentic workflows, multi-turn chat, and coding assistance at lower latency than full-scale frontier models.

Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro) is Google's highest-quality image generation model as of November 2025, designed for professional-grade image editing and generation with advanced reasoning capabilities, improved accuracy, and enhanced image quality, supporting complex and multi-turn image generation and editing, and featuring superior text rendering and world knowledge grounding via Google Search.
Google's first natively multimodal embedding model mapping text, images, video, audio, and PDFs into a single unified vector space for cross-modal semantic search and retrieval .

DeepSeek-V4-Flash is a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language model with 284 billion total parameters and 13 billion activated parameters, designed for efficient reasoning across a 1M-token context window. It uses a hybrid attention mechanism combining Compressed Sparse Attention (CSA) and Heavily Compressed Attention (HCA) to dramatically improve long-context efficiency, requiring only 10% of KV cache compared with DeepSeek-V3.2 at 1M context length. It supports three reasoning modes: Non-think (fast), Think High (logical analysis), and Think Max (full reasoning extent).
DeepSeek's flagship Mixture-of-Experts model with 1.6T total parameters (49B activated) supporting million-token context and advanced reasoning capabilities
Azure deployment of the GPT-Chat Latest model from May 2026, a preview version of the GPT-5.5 series optimized for conversational interfaces with chat completion capabilities .
OpenAI's GPT-5.5 Long Context model with 1M token context window and 128K max output, optimized for extended reasoning and large document processing.
OpenAI's GPT-5.4 Pro variant optimized for complex professional tasks with extended reasoning capabilities, supporting up to 272K context length for detailed context awareness.
OpenAI's state-of-the-art multimodal model released on August 7, 2025, optimized for coding and agentic tasks with advanced reasoning and tool-use capabilities .
OpenAI's fifth-generation multimodal large language model released on August 7, 2025, featuring adaptive reasoning, improved coding, and enhanced safety measures.
OpenAI's most advanced reasoning model optimized for complex, high-stakes tasks requiring step-by-step reasoning and instruction following.
Specialized version of GPT-5 optimized for agentic software engineering in Codex, trained on real-world coding tasks for interactive sessions and long complex tasks with code review capabilities
Lightweight version of GPT-5 optimized for cost-sensitive applications, providing reduced latency and cost while maintaining core instruction-following and safety features.

The smallest and fastest variant in the GPT-5 system, optimized for developer tools, rapid interactions, and ultra-low latency environments. While limited in reasoning depth compared to its larger counterparts, it retains key instruction-following and safety features.

OpenAI's most powerful reasoning model with leading performance on coding, math, science, and vision. Excels at complex queries requiring multi-faceted analysis, especially in programming, business consulting, and creative ideation.
OpenAI's compact o-series reasoning model from April 2025 with native vision support and tool use capabilities, optimized for fast, cost-efficient performance in math, coding, and visual tasks.
OpenAI's GPT-4.1 released April 14, 2025, offering major improvements in coding, instruction following, and long-context comprehension with a 1 million token context window.

OpenAI's GPT-4.1 mini model released April 14, 2025, featuring a 1 million token context window and optimized performance for low-latency tasks while matching or exceeding GPT-4o in intelligence evaluations .

OpenAI's fastest and cheapest model with a 1 million token context window, ideal for low-latency tasks like classification and autocompletion, scoring 80.1% on MMLU and supporting vision and function calling.

OpenAI's first natively multimodal image generation model that can accept both text and image inputs while producing high-quality image outputs, released via the OpenAI API in April 2025 and built on the GPT-4o architecture .

OpenAI's enhanced reasoning model released December 2024 with improved accuracy on math and programming tasks, using 60% fewer tokens for reasoning than o1-preview while maintaining superior performance on complex problem-solving benchmarks.
OpenAI's first o-series reasoning model preview released on September 12, 2024, optimized for complex scientific, mathematical, and coding tasks through internal chain-of-thought reasoning.

OpenAI's first small reasoning model supporting function calling, Structured Outputs, and developer messages, optimized for STEM tasks with three reasoning effort levels (low/medium/high); lacks vision capabilities.
OpenAI's cost-efficient reasoning model optimized for STEM tasks, released September 12, 2024, with 128K context window and 65.5K max output tokens.
OpenAI's most powerful open-weight model, fitting into a single H100 GPU with 117B parameters and 5.1B active parameters, designed for advanced reasoning and agentic tasks.
Azure deployment of GPT-audio-1.5, a specialized audio processing model supporting speech-to-text and text-to-speech capabilities with 128K context window.
OpenAI's production-ready speech-to-speech model for real-time conversational interactions, supporting text, audio input, and audio output with low-latency via WebRTC or WebSocket.

A cost-efficient version of GPT Realtime - capable of responding to audio and text inputs in realtime over WebRTC, WebSocket, or SIP connections. It is a native speech-to-speech model that handles audio input and output directly, eliminating the need for separate transcription and speech synthesis models, thus reducing latency and making conversations feel more fluid and human.
OpenAI's first generally available audio model supporting both audio input and output, enabling voice agents and speech processing in a single API call.
A cost-efficient audio-processing model accepting both text and audio inputs, optimized for voice agents and real-time applications with built-in speech-to-text and text-to-speech capabilities.

Beta preview of Anthropic's computer use capability allowing Claude to interact with desktop environments through mouse/keyboard control and screenshot analysis, available on Claude 3.5 Sonnet (October 2024 snapshot).

OpenAI's Realtime API model optimized for low-latency voice and text conversations, supporting native speech-to-speech processing with improved reasoning and tool use capabilities for production voice agents.
OpenAI's real-time preview model for GPT-4o-Mini, optimized for low-latency voice and multimodal interactions with 128K context window and 16K output token limit.
OpenAI's Realtime API preview model from October 2024 supporting low-latency speech-to-speech conversations with native audio processing, function calling, and image input capabilities .
OpenAI's December 2024 snapshot of the GPT-4o Audio model with reduced audio token costs, supporting native speech input/output in the Chat Completions API.

OpenAI's cost-efficient audio-capable preview model supporting text, vision, and audio inputs with 128K context and 16K output tokens, optimized for real-time voice interactions and multimodal generation.
OpenAI's latest GPT-4o snapshot released November 20, 2024, supporting vision and function calling with a 128K context window and 16.4K output tokens.
OpenAI's August 2024 snapshot of GPT-4o with enhanced multilingual capabilities, structured output support, and improved audio understanding compared to earlier GPT-4o versions.
OpenAI's initial release of the GPT-4o multimodal model from May 13, 2024, supporting text, vision, and audio inputs with native tool use capabilities.
OpenAI's cost-efficient small model supporting text and vision, released July 18, 2024, with 128K context and 16K output tokens.

OpenAI's state-of-the-art text-to-image model with superior prompt understanding, text rendering, and detailed image generation capabilities, natively integrated with ChatGPT for conversational prompt refinement .
OpenAI's second-generation text-to-image model using a diffusion architecture conditioned on CLIP embeddings, generating higher-resolution and more realistic images than its predecessor DALL·E 1.
Legacy Azure OpenAI embedding model based on OpenAI's Ada architecture, optimized for converting text into numerical vector representations for similarity analysis and other NLP tasks.

OpenAI's most capable embedding model for both English and non-English tasks, creating embeddings with up to 3072 dimensions for superior performance in multilingual retrieval and semantic understanding .
OpenAI's efficient small embedding model with 5x lower pricing than ada-002, optimized for multilingual performance and cost-effective embedding tasks .
AI21 Labs' hybrid SSM-Transformer model with 398B total parameters and a 256K context window, optimized for complex reasoning tasks across long documents.

AI21 Labs' lightweight hybrid SSM-Transformer model with 52B total parameters and a 256K context window, optimized for low-latency enterprise tasks and supporting function calling/tool use, structured output (JSON), and grounded generation with citation mode and documents API.

Mid-sized Jurassic-2 LLM from AI21 Labs offering a balance of quality and affordability for a wide range of text generation tasks, optimized for generating precise text from instruction-only prompts with zero-shot capabilities.

AI21 Labs Jurassic-2 Ultra base model with zero-shot instruction capabilities, supporting multiple languages including English, Spanish, French, German, Portuguese, Italian, and Dutch, optimized for production use with reduced latency.

AI21 Labs' instruction-tuned variant of the Jamba hybrid SSM-Transformer model, optimized for enterprise applications with a 256K context window and enhanced safety features for reliable commercial use.

Amazon's lightweight multimodal model supporting text, image, and video inputs with a 1M token context window, optimized for high-volume applications requiring vision capabilities but without tool calling functionality

Amazon's first any-to-any multimodal model that processes text, images, video, and speech inputs while natively generating both text and images, enabling unified reasoning across modalities without model switching.

Amazon Nova Pro is a balanced multimodal model offering strong accuracy, speed, and cost for a wide range of tasks across text, images, and video. It supports extended thinking and tool use for complex reasoning and agentic workflows.

Amazon Nova Micro is a text-only model optimized for speed and low cost, delivering the lowest latency responses in the Amazon Nova family for tasks like text summarization, translation, content classification, interactive chat, brainstorming, and simple mathematical reasoning and coding.
Amazon's low-cost multimodal model for simple automation, document processing, and customer support across text, images, and video with a 1M-token context window and built-in tools.

Amazon Nova Pro is a state-of-the-art multimodal model with advanced capabilities in processing text, image, and video inputs, supporting tool use and vision, optimized for accuracy, speed, and cost across diverse tasks like document analysis and visual question answering.

models.model_name: Amazon Nova Pro (w/ latency optimized inference)
models.description: Amazon Nova Pro is a highly capable multimodal model with the best combination of accuracy, speed, and cost for a wide range of tasks, supporting text, images, video, documents, and code inputs.
Amazon's flagship multimodal model with million-token context for complex reasoning, agentic workflows, and serving as teacher for distillation
Amazon's second-generation text embeddings model with configurable output dimensions (256, 512, or 1024) and improved accuracy for retrieval tasks, optimized for RAG and multilingual use cases.
Amazon Bedrock's native reranking model that improves relevance of retrieved documents for RAG workflows by reordering search results based on query relevance scores.
Anthropic's mid-size model with superior intelligence for high-volume uses, such as coding, in-depth research, and agents, featuring a 200K token context window and 64K max output tokens.

Anthropic's fastest and most cost-efficient Claude model with near-frontier coding performance, supporting extended thinking, computer use, and context awareness for high-throughput real-time applications.
Anthropic's flagship Opus model from May 2025 with 200K context window, extended thinking, parallel tool use, and vision support for complex coding and agentic workflows .

Anthropic's most intelligent model to date and the first hybrid reasoning model on the market, capable of producing near-instant responses or extended, step-by-step thinking that is visible to the user. It shows particularly strong improvements in coding and front-end web development.
Anthropic's mid-tier model offering superior intelligence at twice the speed of Claude 3 Opus, with enhanced coding, reasoning, and vision capabilities.

Anthropic's fastest model in the Claude 3.5 family, released November 2024, offering rapid response times for high-interactivity applications like chatbots and real-time code completions while supporting vision and tool use .

Claude 3.5 Sonnet v2 is an upgraded mid-tier model with enhanced coding and reasoning capabilities, supporting computer use for autonomous desktop interaction and offering twice the output token limit of its predecessor.
Anthropic's fastest and most compact model in the Claude 3 family, optimized for speed and efficiency in near-instant responses with vision capabilities for image processing alongside text.

Cohere's unified multimodal embedding model that processes text, images, and mixed content in a single model for search and RAG, supporting 100+ languages and Matryoshka embeddings for flexible dimensionality.
Cohere's Rerank 3.5 model designed to reorder search results for improved relevance with multilingual support and enhanced reasoning capabilities for complex queries.

DeepSeek-R1 is a first-generation reasoning model trained using large-scale reinforcement learning (RL) to solve complex reasoning tasks across domains such as math, code, and language. It incorporates cold-start data before RL and achieves performance comparable to OpenAI-o1 across math, code, and reasoning tasks.
Google's flagship dense 31B-parameter Gemma 4 model supporting text and image inputs with a 256K context window, optimized for coding, reasoning, and agentic workflows on consumer GPUs.

Google's open-weight Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model with 26B total parameters and roughly 4B active per forward pass, built on the Gemini 3 architecture. Supports function-calling, structured JSON output, native vision, and 140+ languages within a context window of 262.1K tokens.
Google's smallest Gemma 4 edge model with effective 2B parameters, supporting text, image, and audio input natively with 128K context window, optimized for mobile and IoT deployments.
Google's compact 4-billion parameter open model with instruction tuning, designed for on-device and edge deployment. Supports both text and image input with a large 128K context window.
Google's 12-billion parameter open model with instruction tuning, supporting text and image inputs with a 128K context window.
Google's largest open multimodal model with 27B parameters, supporting text and image input and generating text output, with a 128K context window and multilingual support for over 140 languages.

Luma AI's flagship video generation model with 10× compute of Ray 1, producing photorealistic video clips (5-9 seconds) from text or images at up to 1080p resolution with native 24 FPS output and coherent motion physics .
AWS Bedrock deployment of Llama 4 Maverick 17B instruction-tuned model with 128 experts and 400B total parameters, supporting multilingual text and image inputs for chat and visual reasoning tasks.
Meta's Llama 4 Scout 17B instruction-tuned model with 16 experts and a 10M token context window, natively supporting text and image inputs for vision-language tasks.

Amazon Bedrock deployment of Meta's Llama 3.2 3B Instruct model, a lightweight instruction-tuned variant optimized for on-device tasks like summarization and instruction following with multilingual dialogue capabilities .

Meta's 11-billion parameter multimodal model optimized for instruction following, supporting both text and image inputs with a 128K context window. Released under the Llama 3.2 Community License for commercial use.
Meta's 90-billion parameter multimodal model supporting text and image inputs for advanced visual reasoning, document understanding, and instruction following with a 128K context window.
Meta's compact 8-billion parameter instruction-tuned model with a 128K context window, optimized for edge deployment and multilingual dialogue use cases in eight languages.
Meta's 70-billion parameter instruction-tuned model with an extended 128K context window, optimized for multilingual dialogue and assistant-like tasks with tool use and code generation capabilities.
Meta's largest open model with 405 billion parameters and a 128K context window, supporting tool use and multilingual tasks.
Amazon Bedrock deployment of Meta's Llama 3.1 Instruct 70B model with latency-optimized inference, offering 128K context window and tool use capabilities for multilingual dialogue tasks.
Amazon Bedrock deployment of Meta's Llama 3 8B instruction-tuned model with 8K context window, optimized for dialogue and instruction-following tasks.
Meta's 70-billion parameter instruction-tuned model with an 8K context window, optimized for dialogue and assistant-like tasks  .
Llama 2 Chat (13B) fine-tuned for dialogue use cases, optimized for helpfulness and safety in conversational settings with supervised fine-tuning and human feedback alignment.
Meta's Llama 2 70B Chat model fine-tuned for conversational use cases, optimized for dialogue and human alignment using supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning with human feedback.
Meta's base Llama 2 13B pretrained model for text generation, trained on 2 trillion tokens with a 4K context window, available on Amazon Bedrock.
Meta's Llama 2 70B pretrained model, the largest variant in the Llama 2 family with 70 billion parameters, supporting a context window of 4096 tokens.

Meta's Llama 2 13B Chat model fine-tuned for conversational use cases, optimized for dialogue and chat interactions with safety and helpfulness alignment through supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning with human feedback.

MiniMax M2 is a compact, high-efficiency large language model optimized for end-to-end coding and agentic workflows. With 10 billion activated parameters (230 billion total), it delivers near-frontier intelligence across general reasoning, tool use, and multi-step task execution while maintaining low latency and deployment efficiency. The model excels in code generation, multi-file editing, compile-run-fix loops, and test-validated repair, showing strong results on SWE-Bench Verified, Multi-SWE-Bench, and Terminal-Bench. It also performs competitively in agentic evaluations such as BrowseComp and GAIA, effectively handling long-horizon planning, retrieval, and recovery from execution errors.

Pixtral Large (25.02) is Mistral AI's 124B parameter multimodal model that combines advanced vision capabilities with powerful language understanding, supporting text and image inputs for complex visual reasoning tasks.

Mistral AI's 123B-parameter dense transformer coding model with a 256K context window, optimized for agentic software engineering tasks including codebase exploration, multi-file orchestration, and automated failure detection .
Mistral AI's reasoning model with chain-of-thought capabilities for solving complex math, coding, and logic problems, supporting text and vision inputs.

A compact 3B-parameter audio-language model built on Ministral-3B with a Whisper-derived encoder, designed for transcription, audio Q&A, summarization, and function-calling from voice across 8+ languages. It retains best-in-class text performance while adding powerful audio understanding capabilities.

Mistral AI's speech-to-text model with 24 billion parameters for high-accuracy transcription and voice understanding, built on Mistral Small 3 with audio input capabilities and 32K token context window.
Mistral AI's 3B parameter edge model optimized for on-device deployment with vision and instruction capabilities, supporting 256K context window and multilingual text/image processing .
AWS Bedrock deployment of Ministral 3 8B Instruct (Dec 2024 snapshot), a compact 8.4B-parameter vision-capable model optimized for edge deployment with strong instruction-following capabilities .
AWS Bedrock deployment of Ministral 14B 3.0 instruct model with 128K context window, supporting tool use and structured outputs for cost-sensitive automations and high-volume workloads

Mistral Large 3 is Mistral AI's 675-billion parameter model with strong performance on coding, reasoning, and multilingual tasks. It is the instruct post-trained version in FP8, fine-tuned for instruction tasks, making it ideal for chat, agentic and instruction based use cases. Designed for reliability and long-context comprehension - It is engineered for production-grade assistants, retrieval-augmented systems, scientific workloads, and complex enterprise workflows.

Kimi K2 Thinking is Moonshot AI's reasoning model with chain-of-thought capabilities for complex problem solving in math, coding, and logic. It supports up to 256K context window and can execute 200-300 sequential tool calls autonomously with native INT4 quantization for efficiency.

NVIDIA Nemotron Nano 2 9B is a hybrid Mamba-Transformer model optimized for efficient reasoning and chat, supporting multiple languages including English, German, Spanish, French, Italian, and Japanese, with context length up to 128K tokens.
NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano 30B A3B is a lightweight, deep reasoning model optimized for high-throughput agents, supporting a 1M token context window and configurable reasoning modes.

NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super 120B A12B is a large language model trained from scratch by NVIDIA, designed for agentic workflows, long-context reasoning, and high-volume workloads such as IT ticket automation. It employs a hybrid Latent Mixture-of-Experts (LatentMoE) architecture with Mamba-2, MoE, and Attention layers, and incorporates Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) layers for faster text generation.
OpenAI's 20B parameter open-source model optimized for efficient text generation and coding at lower compute cost, supporting 128K context and tool use capabilities .

A streamlined 30.5B parameter Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model optimized for advanced code generation, repository-scale understanding, and agentic tool use, supporting a native context length of 256K tokens extendable to 1M with Yarn and designed for instruction-following without thinking mode.

Qwen's 32-billion parameter dense model with hybrid thinking modes for both fast responses and deep reasoning, supporting seamless switching between thinking and non-thinking modes within a single model for optimal performance across various scenarios.

Qwen's 235-billion parameter mixture-of-experts model with 22 billion active parameters, supporting text and code generation with a 128K context window. This version is optimized for instruction following, logical reasoning, math, code, and tool usage, with significant gains in knowledge coverage, long-context reasoning, and alignment with open-ended tasks.

Qwen's largest coding-specialized mixture-of-experts model with 480B total and 35B active parameters, natively supporting 256K token context and extending to 1M tokens, optimized for agentic coding and software engineering tasks.
Alibaba's efficient Mixture-of-Experts model with 80B total and 3B active parameters, optimized for fast, cost-effective inference and ultra-long context handling up to 256K tokens .
Qwen's vision-language mixture-of-experts model processing text and images for visual reasoning and document understanding, with native 256K context and MoE architecture

Open-weight language model designed specifically for coding agents and local development, with 3B activated parameters (80B total) and 256k context length, optimized for agentic coding tasks and IDE integration.

Enterprise LLM optimized for business writing, content generation, and knowledge work with strong instruction following, supporting advanced reasoning, tool-calling, LLM delegation, built-in RAG, code generation, structured outputs, multi-modality, and multilingual support.

OpenAI's first open-weight 20B parameter model with 128K context window, mxfp4 quantization, built-in chain-of-thought reasoning, and native OpenAI tool calling format — optimized for agentic workflows and complex reasoning on Groq's LPU hardware.

OpenAI's flagship 120B open-weight MoE language model designed for powerful reasoning, agentic tasks, and production-scale applications. Optimized to run on a single 80GB GPU through a Mixture-of-Experts architecture, providing configurable reasoning levels and native tool-use capabilities.

Llama 4 Scout (17Bx16E) is a natively multimodal Mixture of Experts (MoE) model with a context window of 131K tokens, purpose-built for processing entire codebases, multi-document corpora, and extended user activity logs in a single inference call. It activates 17 billion parameters out of a total of 109B, using 16 experts per forward pass.

GroqCloud deployment of Qwen3 32B, a dense 32.8 billion parameter causal language model from Alibaba's Qwen3 series supporting the full 131k context window for advanced multilingual reasoning and efficient dialogue across 100+ languages and dialects .
Expressive text-to-speech model from Canopy Labs generating fast, high-quality audio with vocal direction controls like [cheerful] or [whisper], featuring six professionally-trained voice personas.
Specialized text-to-speech model generating authentic Saudi dialect speech with natural pronunciation and regional nuances, optimized for applications requiring high-quality Arabic speech synthesis .
OpenAI's Whisper large-v3 model with 128 Mel frequency bins and Cantonese language token, trained on 5M+ hours of audio for improved multilingual speech recognition and translation .

OpenAI's fastest speech recognition model optimized for speed while maintaining high accuracy, featuring streamlined processing and efficiency improvements for enhanced speed without sacrificing transcription quality.
Moonshot AI's enhanced Kimi K2 Instruct model with 256k context window, improved agentic coding capabilities, and structured output support for tool calling and advanced reasoning tasks.

Pearl Research Labs' instruction-tuned checkpoint of Google's Gemma 4 31B, optimized for the Pearl Network's Proof of Useful Work protocol, delivering capabilities similar to the base Gemma 4 31B — text input, 32K context, function calling, JSON mode — at a 25%+ discount through Together AI's exclusive Pearl Network integration.

Alibaba's flagship Qwen3.7-Max model optimized for agentic workflows and autonomous task execution with a 1-million-token context window, designed for complex coding, long-horizon reasoning, and multi-step tool calling.

Coding-focused agentic model built on Kimi K2.6 architecture with 1T total parameters (32B activated) MoE, 256K context window, and native INT4 quantization. Optimized for long-horizon software engineering tasks with 30% fewer thinking tokens than K2.6.

Alibaba's cost-effective multimodal agent model built on the Qwen3.7 text backbone, supporting text, image, and video input with text output only. Designed as a versatile agent foundation for GUI interaction, screen reading, and code generation from visual references.
Flagship open reasoning model for agentic software engineering with 1M context window, purpose-built for long-horizon tasks and complex coding workflows .
Liquid AI's largest hybrid MoE model with 24B total parameters (2.3B activated per token), optimized for high-volume multi-agent pipelines and efficient on-device deployment with 32K context length .

Z.ai's flagship model for long-horizon agentic engineering with 200K context and 128K output tokens, delivering 28% coding performance improvement over GLM-5 through refined reinforcement learning while maintaining the same 754B parameter MoE architecture.

Kimi's latest and most intelligent model with improved long-horizon coding stability, supporting native multimodal architecture for text, image, and video inputs, thinking and non-thinking modes, and dialogue and Agent tasks.

Alibaba's flagship large language model with hybrid architecture combining linear attention and sparse MoE routing, optimized for agentic coding, frontend development, and complex problem-solving with always-on chain-of-thought reasoning and native multimodal support.

Meta's 70-billion parameter instruction-tuned model optimized for multilingual dialogue with a 128K context window, released December 6, 2024, supporting English, German, French, Italian, Portuguese, Hindi, Spanish, and Thai.

models.description: AI21 Labs' hybrid SSM-Transformer model with 398B total parameters and a 256K context window, optimized for complex reasoning tasks across long documents. Available on Amazon Bedrock with native support for structured JSON output and function calling.

models.description: Google's premium image-generation and editing model built on Gemini 3 Pro, featuring advanced multimodal reasoning, real-world grounding, and high-fidelity visual synthesis with support for up to 14 reference images and strong text-in-image rendering .

DeepSeek's current standard public API alias for DeepSeek-V3.2 in non-thinking mode, optimized for general chat, structured output, tool use, and everyday application workflows with a 128K context window.

Mistral Large 3 is Mistral AI's flagship Mixture-of-Experts model with 675B total parameters (41B active) and a 256K context window, supporting native vision and multilingual capabilities while being fully open-weight under Apache 2.0.

Mistral Medium 3, released May 2025, balances frontier performance with cost efficiency at $0.4 per million input tokens and $2 per million output tokens, supporting hybrid/on-prem deployments and multilingual capabilities.
Mistral Small 4 unifies instruction, reasoning, and coding capabilities with multimodal input in a single 119B MoE model, supporting configurable reasoning modes and 256K context window.

Mistral OCR 3 is the latest iteration of Mistral's OCR service, designed to extract text and embedded images from PDFs and images with exceptional fidelity. It is optimized for complex document layouts, including forms, handwritten notes, tables, and mixed content, and outputs markdown enriched with HTML-based table reconstruction for structured understanding. Unlike earlier versions, it achieves a 74% overall win rate over Mistral OCR 2 across real-world document types, particularly excelling in handling low-quality scans and dense layouts. The model is available as a hosted API and is selectively deployable on-premises for sensitive data scenarios.
Enterprise-ready coding agent model with 256K context window, designed for complex software engineering tasks and agentic workflows.
24B-parameter agentic coding model with vision support, optimized for local deployment and fine-tuned for instruction-following via Mistral's API.

Mistral AI's latest code-specialized LLM supporting 80+ programming languages, optimized for low-latency tasks like fill-in-the-middle completion, code correction, and test generation with 2.5x faster performance than its predecessor.

Mistral's first reasoning model with native chain-of-thought, function calling, and structured output capabilities, optimized for complex multi-step problem solving with transparent reasoning traces across languages and domains.
A reasoning model optimized for complex, multi-step problem solving with function calling and structured output capabilities, supporting both text and vision inputs.
Ultra-compact 3B parameter model optimized for edge deployment, supporting 256K context window and vision capabilities for efficient on-device AI.
Mistral's 8B parameter edge-optimized model with a 128K context window, supporting tool use and function calling for efficient local deployment.
Mistral AI's 14-billion parameter dense language model optimized for edge deployment, supporting vision, multilingual capabilities, and advanced reasoning with a large 256K context window.

Mistral's open-weight text-to-speech model with zero-shot voice cloning, supporting 9 languages and generating lifelike speech for voice agents with 70ms latency and 3-second voice cloning from reference audio .
Efficient transcription-optimized version of Voxtral Mini designed specifically for audio-to-text conversion with enhanced latency-efficiency on Mistral's API.
Voxtral Small is Mistral AI's first model with native audio input capabilities for instruct use cases, built on Mistral Small 3 with 24B parameters and Apache 2.0 license.
Mistral's 3B classifier model optimized for text classification and moderation tasks, offering efficient and accurate content analysis.
Mistral's latest moderation model with 128k context window and jailbreaking detection, released March 2026 and replacing the deprecated mistral-moderation-2411.
Mistral's latest moderation model with 128k context window and jailbreaking detection, optimized for complex multilingual data and long multi-turn conversations .
Mistral AI's first embedding model specialized for code, designed for high-performance code retrieval and semantic understanding with state-of-the-art accuracy on real-world code data .
Mistral's dedicated text embedding model that generates fixed-length 1024-dimension vector representations of text for semantic analysis, clustering, and retrieval applications .

State-of-the-art 12B parameter model built with NVIDIA, featuring a 128K context window and the Tekken tokenizer trained on 100+ languages, excelling in multilingual tasks, coding, and reasoning as a drop-in replacement for Mistral 7B.
